# Feature Saliency Audit Report

*Generated by `scripts/feature_saliency_audit.py`*

## Methodology

- **Data**: 2000 bars of synthetic OHLCV with 3 known regimes (bear/sideways/bull), each with distinct drift and volatility.
- **Engine**: FSHMM (n_states=3, saliency_threshold=0.0, max_iter=40)
- **Runs**: 5 independent runs with different random seeds (42–46)
- **Features**: Full generic feature set via `add_features()` (see `feature_engineering.py`)
- **Metric**: Saliency weight ρₖ ∈ [0, 1] — 1 = highly relevant, 0 = irrelevant background

## Category Summary

| Category | Mean ρ | Std ρ | # Features |
|----------|--------|-------|------------|
| Returns | 0.4641 | 0.0012 | 2 |
| Momentum | 0.4243 | 0.4236 | 2 |
| Volume | 0.3405 | 0.1972 | 4 |
| Moving Averages | 0.2195 | 0.3788 | 4 |
| Time-based | 0.1427 | 0.2231 | 17 |
| Volatility | 0.0007 | 0.0000 | 6 |
| Trend | 0.0007 | 0.0000 | 3 |

## Recommendations

- Features with mean ρ < 0.3 across multiple runs are candidates for trimming.
- Time-based features (day_of_week, hour, minute, session, etc.) should be evaluated per data frequency — on daily data they are constant or near-constant and add no signal.
- Before removing any features, re-run this audit on real market data (e.g. BTC/USD, ES futures) as synthetic data may not capture all real-world relationships.

## Caveats

- **Synthetic data**: The regimes are simple drift/vol changes. Real markets exhibit more complex dynamics.
- **No PCA**: This audit was run without PCA whitening, so features are evaluated in their original space.
- **Single asset class**: Results may differ for equities vs crypto vs FX.
- **Feature collinearity**: Highly correlated features may split saliency weight, depressing individual rankings.
- **This is an AUDIT report**. No features have been removed. Use this evidence to inform future trimming decisions.
